text.content:
A Soldier's Duty

A Scenario inspired by The Steadfast Tin Soldier (Hans Christian Andersen)

Safety Keywords: Rape / Revenge / Murder

Once upon a time...

The Hunters arrive in Borgholm, on the beautiful island of Oland. The Hunters' intervention is ordered by the Papacy after a request from the Riksråd, the great council of the kingdom of Sweden. A former soldier and war hero named Nils, also called the Poor Devil, has sent a letter to the council, imploring them to help him and his former comrades. Six valiant soldiers were all that remained of their company after the bloody war between Sweden and Russia. Now, they are being killed one by one by a monster that seems able to take a bullet without batting an eye. Two of the comrades have already fallen to the monster's fury. Those that still live have barricaded themselves in the mansion of their one-time commander, Andersen, who now leads them in defense of their own lives and those of his family.

The story that led...

Six years earlier, a company of Carolean soldiers was stationed in Vaasa, a small town on the Finnish coast. Here Private Enar fell in love with Satu, a young and beautiful dancer. Enar deserted to remain near his beloved but was discovered by his comrades. They judged him a sinner according to the strict religious discipline of the Caroleans. Led by Captain Andersen, the soldiers punished Enar by cutting off his leg and throwing him into the sea. But in reality, the true reason for Andersen's ferocity arose from the fact that the officer too had fallen in love with Satu and, mad with jealousy, had punished Enar far beyond what the rules dictated. After Enar's execution, unknown even to his own men, the Captain tried to romance Satu, who rejected him. Unable to accept that refusal, Andersen raped her and as a result of that terrible act, the girl became pregnant.

Six years later, the company disbanded and Andersen, a wealthy war hero, paid his underlings handsomely to keep the secret of Enar's death. Having retired to private life, over time, Andersen grew to regret his deplorable behavior. He sought out the dancer and, after regaining contact with her, discovered with great shame the existence of his son, Erik. Ravaged by guilt for what he had done, Andersen managed to earn Satu's grudging forgiveness after months of effort. He freed her from a life of hardship as an assistant cook and took her with him to his mansion, together with his son. But Enar's fate did not end beneath the sea waves. Love for Satu soon turned into blind hatred for Andersen and his former comrades. Below the waves, Enar remained alive for a long time while the fish slowly devoured his flesh. Of the soldier's body, only the heart was spared and, as it fell into the abyss, it attracted the attention of a Sea Fairy. The Fairy took pity on him and, seeing that heart so wounded, infused it with new life. Enar's body slowly began to regenerate, but the young soldier's mind was now lost. All the accumulated hatred and resentment had driven him insane. Worse, in the process of his rebirth, the soldier's body began to absorb everything it found. Algae, shells, and timbers from an old wreck fused to his limbs, making him an immortal monster bound to the world only by an infinite grudge...

pages.name: The Story at the Arrival of the Hunters

text.content:
The story at the arrival of the Hunters...

Six years have passed, and Andersen managed to get closer to Satu, who is now at least accepting him. At the same time, Enar's heart has started beating again. Now that his greatest enemy, Andersen, has found something he truly loves and would not want to lose, Enar is ready to return to take it away from him.

Appearing every day an hour after sunset, Enar began his personal war by killing his fellow soldiers, one by one, in a heinous fashion. Six survived the war, and two have already been murdered, with one gutted and another burned alive after being flayed. Andersen soon understood the nature of the threat and brought Hans and Christian, his remaining former companions, to his mansion. Only Nils was not called, being unable to move due to his war wounds. The Poor Devil has many friends, however, and has gathered information from his home in Sundsvall.

Enar is completely insane and fully intent on killing all his former comrades, only to finally "merge" into one being with his beloved Satu. Nils in his heart sensed that the killer is Enar and, seized by a sense of guilt, he summoned the Order to try and stop his former comrade in arms. Andersen has found happiness with Satu and Erik, and despite the shame he feels for what he has done in the past, he is absolutely determined to destroy the revived Enar to secure a future with his new family. Satu has guessed who the monstrous being that appears every night really is, but fear makes her hide from Enar's gaze. The woman now wants nothing more than the best for her child, even to the detriment of her own happiness, even if it means accepting a life alongside the one who took away her true love six years earlier. On this sad stage, all the actors have something to hide while Enar represents the truth that, inescapably, will destroy the wall of lies built with care by Andersen and his men.

Main NPCs

Andersen

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a war veteran with strong leadership skills. In addition to being a good leader, I am also a capable fighter.

Agenda: Defend my new family from the danger that Enar represents / Redeem myself in the eyes of Satu and make her understand that I am now a different man / Prevent anyone from discovering what I have done.

Gift - Commander: Andersen is skilled at giving orders and managing a battle. Up to 3 times per Scene, Andersen can issue an order to an ally who will receive 1 Additional Success (+1 to OL in the case of an NPC) while executing it.

Enar

OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (4 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am an immortal being, the result of all the hatred I harbored in my exile into the abyss. I have the strength of five men and my body feels no pain.

Agenda: Take revenge on Andersen, but not before I have eliminated all my former companions / After eliminating Andersen and his people, I want to meld with Satu so that our bodies and souls are united for eternity.

Gift - The Eternal Soldier: Enar's body is the result of powerful magic tainted by hatred. When Enar suffers his final Wound, his body becomes seawater and soaks into the ground. The following day, Enar completely regenerates himself at the bottom of the sea, where his heart is, the very focus of his existence. Only by recovering the heart and destroying it can Enar be permanently eliminated.

Gift - Rage of the Sea: Thanks to the magic granted by the Sea Fairy, Enar has gained powers. When he acts, he can always choose an additional effect. 1. Algae Whip: A string of algae stretches from Enar's body and can perform a second independent action. 2. Wave: Enar's body becomes seawater for a few moments, allowing him to slip away and cover a distance of 30 feet before regrouping. 3. Shells: 3 large seashells are shot from Enar's body in different directions, which should be considered 3 attacks with OL Medium 5. The shells must be aimed at 3 different targets.

Satu

OL Easy 3 – Main NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a former dancer who has lost everything. I am but a shadow of my former self.

Agenda: Defend my son Erik, the only reason that I keep going.

Nils the Poor Devil

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am an old soldier who lost his legs in the war.

Agenda: The guilt for what we did to Enar haunts me, I want to find a way to bring him peace.

Sea Fairy

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a fairy of the sea and my magic allows me to perform true miracles. My entire existence is tied to the sea and leaving it would destroy me.

Agenda: I am sad because Enar has forsaken the love that kept him alive in favor of blind hatred. I would like to do something for him, but I cannot go to the mainland to stop his fury or find his beloved.

Special Agenda (Marina the Sea Explorer): I have heard of Marina and know her reputation. She uses her powers only to enrich herself at the expense of others. I will never agree to help her unless she first shows me that she is also capable of good deeds.

Gift – Sea Magic: The Sea Fairy can always use one of the following effects. 1. Water of Life: A target recovers 1 Wound and, if they have been dead for no more than a day, they can even come back to life. 2. The Fury of the Sea: The water of the sea engulfs everything, sweeping away all threats. Those who are within 30 feet must pass a Defense Check against OL Hard 7 to avoid being overwhelmed by the waves and being washed away, suffering 1 Wound. The Sea Fairy can deliberately avoid hitting anyone in the area of effect. 3. The Waves of the Sea: The Sea Fairy can travel to any point of the sea within 6 miles. This shift is instantaneous, and if she wants, the Fairy can take up to 2 subjects with her.

Supporting NPCs

Christian

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a former soldier, called the Relentless by all because of my cold determination. I act without any qualms when it comes to executing an order.

Agenda: Help Andersen in memory of the old days and a military life I can't forget / Eliminate Enar to prove I am the better man.

Gift - The Relentless: When he is carrying out an order, Christian is immune to any effect that affects his behavior, such as fear or charm. The soldier increases his Opposition Level by 1 when he tries to eliminate an obstacle that stands between him and the subject of an order he must carry out.

Erik

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a lively child who can never sit still.

Agenda: Understand who Andersen really is, and why he took my mom and me out of the misery we lived in.

Hans

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (4 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a former soldier, once one of the company's best marksmen. I never miss my mark and always have a loaded weapon hidden under my heavy overcoat.

Agenda: Convince Nils to keep our secret or remove him.

Gift - Gunslinger: Hans owns a pair of prized experimental pistols, which he bought from an inventor for more than half of his war earnings. Hans's guns can fire multiple shots at the same time and with one attack, the soldier can aim at 3 targets that are within an area of 30 feet square. While firing one pistol, Hans can reload the other as an additional action. When he draws a pistol from under his coat, Hans can make an attack with OL Hard 7.

Gift - Smoke Bombs: Hans has some smoke bombs, which he uses to surprise the enemy. While firing a pistol, Hans can drop a bomb as an additional action. The thick smoke will cover an area of 30 feet square and inside it, everyone receives 1 Penalty for every action that requires the use of sight, except for Hans, who is immune to the effect. Additionally, he can always get away from opponents who engage him without anyone preventing him.

Dead Pirates

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a lost soul who only wishes for revenge on those who are still alive. My body is a heap of swollen and rotten flesh that moves slowly.

Special agenda (James the Swordsman): We recognize James as the one who killed our captain in a duel! It is his fault that our ship was wrecked, and we will taste his flesh first!

Gift - Walking Dead: The body of the Dead Pirate is rotten and putrescent, but it does not stop even if dismembered. After suffering his only Wound, a Dead Pirate can still take his action before being eliminated, if he has not already done so in the course of the Exchange. If a Dead Pirate acts against an outnumbered enemy, his Opposition Level is increased by 1.

Key Locations

Andersen's Estate

Descriptor: A large, lush estate ten minutes from the city center.

Information: Wandering around the estate, the Hunters may encounter one or more of these NPCs. Roll a die:

  1. 1-2 Satu: With a sad and depressed air, the woman will try to avoid any interaction with the Hunters. Her shy demeanor will make it clear that something is bothering her.
  2. 3-4 Erik: Intrigued by the Hunters, he will approach them brimming with questions. He is a lively child, and it will soon become clear to the Hunters that he is not fully aware of the danger he is in.
  3. 5-6 Adam the Chatty Servant: A young man in his 20s who can't escape to the city, as he works on the estate to pay off a debt to Andersen. Adam will speak without much hesitation about the fact that there is a complex relationship between his master and Satu, and that the woman comes from the past of the former commander. Adam will add that Andersen is also very considerate of Erik and it becomes clear that he regards the boy as a son.

Over twenty servants work on the estate, but few are capable of wielding a weapon. The whole defense rests on the shoulders of Andersen and Christian. It will be clear to the Hunters, however, that Andersen's abilities as a commander allow him to keep everything under control and to bring out the best even from inexperienced servants. After the first encounter with Enar, Erik will recognize the shells he collected when he lived with his mother in a small cliffside village. According to some old stories, under the cliff there is a dense network of caves where a Sea Fairy is said to reside.

Sundsvall

Descriptor: A small coastal town, where the tranquillity of the sea provides peace and serenity.

Information: It's easy to find old Nils just by wandering around the town. The Poor Devil has invested himself in the community by donating part of his wealth to the neediest, and he is well regarded by all in town. In Nils' house rests a diary where the former soldier wrote down everything that happened six years earlier. From the Poor Devil's memories, it is possible to reconstruct Enar's identity and his relationship with Satu, but not that Erik is Andersen's son. The abuse perpetrated by the officer was never discovered by his comrades in arms. However, it will be clear from the writings that the punishment reserved for Enar was beyond reason.

The Cliff

Descriptor: A steep cliff overlooking the sea, riddled with many inlets. In the most inaccessible part, there are entrances that lead to underground caves.

Information: Going down the cliff, it is possible to arrive at the caves' entrance. In the center of the tunnel network, there is a pool where the Sea Fairy lives. Getting to the pool may require you to deal with one or more of these Threats. Roll a die:

  1. 1-2 Landslide: As they descend along the cliffs, part of the rock path collapses under the Hunters' feet. Descriptor: A sudden landslide. OL Hard 7. In the landslide, a Hunter can suffer 1 Wound or lose some of their equipment to the sea.
  2. 3-4 Submerged tunnel: To get to the central pool you need to swim through a narrow tunnel. Descriptor: A long, narrow submerged tunnel. OL Medium 5. Failing the Check means not being able to make it through the tunnel successfully and returning to the entrance.
  3. 5-6 Hook's Crew: A dozen pirates died here after a shipwreck and now their souls are demanding vengeance on the living.

Scenario Events

In this Scenario, there are eight Events. As Storyteller feel free to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.

Note: This Scenario uses Events as timers for story development. For this reason, the Storyteller must keep in mind some factors.

  • There are only 3 places (the Andersen Estate, the town of Sundsvall, and the cliff) that make up an equilateral "triangle". Moving from one place to another always takes 1 day of travel on foot.
  • Some Events have an indicative day number in which they occur: 1st day, 2nd day, and so on. In this way, the Storyteller will always know where Enar will be and who he will try to strike.
  • Since the action always occurs in two places at the same time, the Storyteller must point out that all the former soldiers are in danger, and that this mission requires action on multiple fronts. For this reason, splitting the Hunters into two groups is encouraged. However, it is not an essential condition and the final decision regarding what to do will always be up to the Players.

Day 1: Enar will attack Andersen's estate shortly after sunset. If the Hunters are not present, Christian will be killed.

Day 1: Hans arrives in Sundsvall to interrogate Nils, who will no longer be willing to keep quiet about Enar's murder. If the Hunters are not present, the soldier will kill the Poor Devil.

Day 2: Enar will arrive after sunset in Sundsvall to punish Nils. If the latter is already dead, then he will find Hans ready to ambush him. If the Hunters are not present, Hans will die. If Nils is alive, however, Enar will attempt to eliminate him unless the Hunters do something to prevent it.

Day 2: Erik will recognize the shells that have fallen from Enar's body as those present on the cliff. If the Hunters are present, Andersen will invite them to investigate. Otherwise he will send Christian, who will be eliminated by the Sea Fairy.

Day 3: Enar will arrive at the estate after sunset to eliminate Andersen and possibly Christian. Once this is done, he will try to reach Satu.

If Erik is put in danger: Both Andersen and Satu will put their lives at risk for him. Both are willing to confess if this can save the child from danger.

If Enar discovers that Erik is Andersen's son: The child will become his new target, as killing him is the best way to make Andersen feel the pain he has inflicted.

If the Hunters convince the Sea Fairy of their good intentions: The Fairy will lead one of them to the bottom of the sea where Enar's heart is.

Exclusive Scenario Gifts

These Gifts represent special abilities related to the A Soldier's Duty Scenario and can be acquired once the requirements are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous one.

Blessing of the Sea

Requirement: Prove to the Sea Fairy your intention to do something for poor Enar.

You are blessed by the mighty Sea Fairy and therefore sea-related abilities have no effect on you. You receive 2 Additional Successes on each Defense Check you make to defend yourself against a Gift, spell, or action made using a Descriptor that includes the word sea.

Art of Command

Requirement: Withstand an onslaught from Enar by fighting alongside Andersen.

You've observed a good leader and figured out how to hone your strategies. Once per Scene, you can give an order to one of your allies and if they try to carry it out, they will receive 2 Additional Successes to any Check.

Coral of the Sea Fairy (Special gift for Marina the Sea Explorer)

Requirement: To kill the Sea Fairy.

You dispatched that hateful fairy and harnessed her soul in a lump of coral. Now her power is yours. You can use 1 Treasure Token to unlock this Gift for the duration of the Scenario. Spend 1 Soma to tap into the Coral's power and choose one of the following effects. 1. Water of Life: A target recovers 1 Wound. 2. The Fury of the Sea: The water of the sea engulfs everything, sweeping away all threats. Those who are within 30 feet must pass a Defense Check against OL Hard 7 to avoid being overwhelmed by the waves and being thrown away, suffering 1 Wound. 3. The Waves of the Sea: You can travel to any point of the sea within 6 miles. This shift is instantaneous and you can take up to 2 subjects with you.

Sea creatures sense what you have done and will attack you without hesitation to try to free the soul of the Fairy.

The Scenario in Scenes

This scenario follows a dynamic structure where the Hunters must manage multiple threats simultaneously. The story unfolds across three locations forming a triangle: Andersen's Estate, Sundsvall, and the Cliff.

FIRST SCENE – The Arrival at the Estate: The Hunters arrive at Andersen's fortified mansion and learn about the threat. Andersen suggests splitting forces to protect both the estate and Nils in Sundsvall.

SECOND SCENE OPTION A – The Defense: Hunters at the estate face Enar's first attack after sunset. During the battle, Erik accidentally reveals he is Andersen and Satu's son. Erik recognizes shells from the cliff on Enar's body.

SECOND SCENE OPTION B – The Poor Devil: Hunters in Sundsvall arrive to find Hans attempting to execute Nils to keep secrets. If Nils survives or the Hunters find his diary, they learn the truth about Enar's punishment but not about Andersen's rape of Satu.

THIRD SCENE OPTION A – The Cliff: Following Erik's clue, Hunters descend the dangerous cliff to find the Sea Fairy's cave. The Fairy reveals Enar's heart lies at the bottom of the sea and can be retrieved if the Hunters bring Satu to say goodbye to Enar's heart.

THIRD SCENE OPTION B – Return to the Estate: Hunters return to find another attack by Enar. After the battle, Andersen confesses everything. Satu flees to the cliff to confront Enar alone.

FOURTH SCENE – Destinies: The final confrontation at the cliff. Enar attempts to merge with Satu. The Hunters must stop him and can only permanently kill him by destroying his heart with the Sea Fairy's help. The fate of Enar, Satu, Andersen, and Erik rests in the Hunters' hands.

flags.inspiration: The Steadfast Tin Soldier by Hans Christian Andersen
